detail: foreach (double v in values) { ... } accumulates count/sum/sumSquares/min/max imperatively. CSP0001 ImperativeControlFlow violation in Domain scope (Core.Runtime). The function is internal but lives at libs/csharp/core/Runtime — analyzer's IsDomainOrApplication flag is true.
fix: Replace with values.Fold over a six-tuple seed (count,sum,sumSq,min,max,allFinite); or use values.Sum + values.Min + values.Max + values.Map(v => v*v).Sum + values.ForAll(RhinoMath.IsValidDouble) and feed the four scalars into Computed. Both LanguageExt Seq operators are O(n) so the algorithmic complexity is preserved.

detail: The same foreach also performs five reassignments per iteration on local mutable doubles/ints (count++, sum +=, sumSquares +=, minimum =, maximum =, allFinite &&). CSP0718 MutableAccumulatorInLoop is the closer match for the data-flow pattern; CSP0707 VariableReassignment also applies to the inner reassignments.
fix: Same as C-01 — fold-based replacement removes both the imperative loop and the reassignment chain in a single edit.

detail: Variables count, sum, sumSquares, minimum, maximum, allFinite are reassigned via ++, +=, =, && inside the loop body. CSP0707 forbids reassignment-driven pipelines.

Computed is a single-call private static helper invoked from one site (line 50). CSP0503 SingleUsePrivateHelper applies. The function is the trailing arithmetic of StatsOf; inlining it removes one function and the .Match(Succ:..., Fail:...) channel adapter, since Computed always succeeds.
fix: Inline the four arithmetic lines into the success arm of the StatsOf switch. Drops Computed (13 LOC) and the Match wrapper at lines 50-53 (4 LOC).

detail: GeometryRequirement is a [Flags] enum exposing 8 bit flags + 8 named compositions. The audit found 102 callsites across libs/csharp/analysis, tests, and Core itself. ZERO callsites perform direct bitwise composition on the bit flags — every callsite uses a named alias (Basic, CurveLength, AreaMass, VolumeMass, SurfaceEvaluation, MeshCheck, SolidTopology, StrictStructure, Strict, None). The bit flags are private vocabulary used only inside the file. The bitmask abstraction is therefore unused at the call surface; only the named compositions are.
fix: Convert GeometryRequirement to [SmartEnum<string>] with one value per named composition, OR convert to a sealed DU (sealed abstract record GeometryRequirement) with one case per named composition. Each case carries an immutable Seq<GeometryCheck> identifying which checks apply. The check dispatcher (GeometryCheck.Apply at line 348) becomes a simple `requirement.Checks.Contains(this)` projection. CSP0001/CSP0707 risk in Includes (line 142-143) disappears since bitwise & on enum is no longer needed.

detail: GeometryCheck is currently a sealed class with private constructor and 13 internal static fields, each capturing four parameters (key, requirement, applies, validate). The class effectively models a closed family. Combined with the [Flags] vocabulary, the dispatch fold at lines 28-45 evaluates ALL 13 checks for every call regardless of requirement, then short-circuits inside Apply via Includes — wasted lambda evaluations on the static field reference path. Collapsing requirement into the check itself eliminates the `requirement` parameter from Apply and lets the dispatcher project `Strict.Checks` once and fold only the relevant subset.
fix: Two-step: (1) make GeometryCheck the discriminated case (sealed abstract record GeometryCheck + 13 sealed records, each with its own Apply override or a Switch via Thinktecture [Union]); (2) make GeometryRequirement carry Seq<GeometryCheck>. Validate becomes `requirement.Checks.Aggregate(...)`. The applies + requirement gate collapses into the Match arm of each case. Estimated reduction: ~80 LOC (the static field declarations become record cases at ~5 LOC each instead of ~14, and the dispatcher loses its requirement parameter, the [Flags] enum, the Includes extension, and the always-evaluated 13-element Seq All).

detail: `(self & requirement) == requirement` is the canonical .NET enum.HasFlag idiom. .NET 5+ enum.HasFlag is jit-intrinsified — there is no perf reason to write the bitwise expression. The extension is single-use (one callsite, line 349) and would disappear under the C-06 collapse.
fix: Either (a) replace with self.HasFlag(requirement) and remove the extension class, or (b) collapse under C-06 and the entire concern vanishes.

detail: Five repeated arms (Plane/Line/Circle/Arc/Sphere) each pattern-match `xxx.IsValid` and project to `Fin.Succ/Fin.Fail` with the same shape. The Native operand check is conceptually identical to RhinoMatch.RequireValid (libs/csharp/core/Runtime/RhinoMatch.cs:11-45) but expressed via Validation rather than Fin. The two functions duplicate validity-by-shape dispatch logic across 51 + 24 = 75 LOC.
fix: Delegate to RhinoMatch.RequireValid by lifting the operand into a synthetic OperationKey, OR introduce a polymorphic `IRhinoValid<T> { bool IsValid }` projection that both consume. Saves ~15 LOC and removes the duplication that will rot when new Rhino primitives are added.

detail: FromDocument accepts a nullable RhinoDoc?. Inside the function it pulls candidate.ModelAbsoluteTolerance, ModelRelativeTolerance, ModelAngleToleranceRadians, ModelUnitSystem, GetCustomUnitSystem(...). RhinoDoc is a Rhino SDK boundary primitive being read by Domain code. The analyzer scope is Domain (file path libs/csharp/core/Domain). This is a controlled boundary — the data is read once into the constructor and never persisted — but it should be marked with the [BoundaryImperativeExemption] attribute or moved behind an adapter in libs/csharp/grasshopper. No active CSP0101 violation because the call uses no statement forms, but the architectural boundary is unsigned.
fix: Either (a) add [BoundaryAdapter] attribute to FromDocument, (b) move FromDocument to a Boundary-scoped file (e.g., libs/csharp/grasshopper/RhinoBridge.cs), or (c) accept a pre-projected DocumentSnapshot record at the boundary and let Domain consume only that.

line.detail: Four [ValueObject<double>] partial structs each declare an identical pattern: an internal `double Value => _value` projection plus a static `Create(double)` returning Fin<T> with a 2-arm finite/range guard. The wrappers differ only in the (range-predicate, label, requirement-message) triple. The cumulative declaration cost is ~64 LOC for what is conceptually one polymorphic shape.
line.fix: Introduce a single generic [ValueObject<double>] struct ToleranceScalar<TKind> where TKind : struct, IToleranceKind (a marker type-class supplying predicate + label). Or, given the call sites all originate in CreateDefault/FromDocument/FromKnownUnits (all in this file), inline the predicates into Create directly and emit four named static factories on a single ToleranceValue type. Saves ~30 LOC.

line.detail: `Combine` and `KeepFirst` are thin wrappers over LanguageExt's `Apply` on a ValueTuple of Validation<Error, T>. LanguageExt 5 exposes the same combinators as `(va, vb).Apply((a, b) => (a, b))` and `(va, vside).Apply((a, _) => a)` directly. The wrappers add no domain semantics and are single-use indirection.
line.fix: Inline both calls at their use sites in GeometryValidation.cs (line 52, 58, 65) and delete ValidationCombinators. Saves 12 LOC and removes a forwarding boundary.

line.detail: `ToEff` overloads adapt Fin<T> -> Eff<AnalysisRuntime, T> and Validation<Error, T> -> Eff<AnalysisRuntime, T>. LanguageExt 5 already provides implicit conversions Fin<T> => Eff<RT, T> via the K<F, A> applicative; the explicit ToEff is renaming the same forward-conversion. Verify by removing the second overload and seeing if call sites in libs/csharp/analysis still type-check; if so, this is canonical wrapper-renaming forbidden by the CSP discipline.
line.fix: Remove ToEff(Fin<T>) since `effect.As()` and direct return achieve the same. Keep ToEff(Validation<...>) only if `validation.ToFin()` cannot be implicit at call sites.

line.detail: No rule covers `[Flags]` enum overuse. CSP0708 covers method family inflation and CSP0702 covers DU shape, but `[Flags]` enums with >N callsites and zero direct bitwise composition (i.e., the enum is being abused as a SmartEnum) is unflagged. GeometryRequirement is exactly such an instance.
line.fix: Add CSP0721 'FlagsEnumNoBitwiseComposition': flag `[Flags]` enum declarations whose external callsites never perform `|` or `&` on the bit-flag members and only reference named alias compositions. Detect via Roslyn IPropertyReferenceOperation/IBinaryOperation analysis on consumers.

line.detail: No rule explicitly forbids `Aggregate` over a Seq when the seed is a tuple containing a Validation/Fin and the loop body re-applies the validation. This is the canonical 'fold-with-Result-tuple' pattern that GeometryValidation.Validate uses (line 27-45). The pattern is acceptable but error-prone; without an analyzer it can degrade into mutable accumulation hidden behind ValueTuple.
line.fix: Add CSP0722 'AggregateValidationFold': suggest `TraverseFin`/`TraverseValidation` (LanguageExt 5 ships these) when an Aggregate seed contains a Fin<T> or Validation<E,T> and the lambda body Apply-projects them. Sister rule to CSP0710 FilterMapChainOnSeq.

line.detail: No rule covers `RhinoCommon` SDK boundary leakage in Domain scope. Rhino types (RhinoDoc, GeometryBase, BoundingBox, Plane, etc.) are domain-allowed because Core models geometry, but specific volatile boundary primitives — RhinoDoc.ActiveDoc, RhinoApp.MainWindow, RhinoApp.RunScript — are imperative state escape hatches. The analyzer's BoundaryNamespace/BoundaryPath markers cover .Boundary, .Rhino, .Adapter directories but the analyzer cannot detect a Domain file that calls RhinoDoc.ActiveDoc.
line.fix: Add CSP0723 'RhinoActiveDocLeak' (or a generalised 'StaticAmbientStateAccess') flagging static-property reads on a configured allowlist of well-known boundary singletons (RhinoDoc.ActiveDoc, etc.) when the calling scope is Domain.

line.detail: OperationFault and SemanticFault are two parallel static error factory groups. SemanticFault has 2 members (PrimitiveNoEdges, PrimitiveNoVertices). OperationFault has 5 members (MissingOperation, MissingContext, InvalidInput, InvalidResult, Unsupported). The split into two static classes is purely categorical — both produce LanguageExt Error values keyed by OperationKey. The semantic distinction (operation-shape vs primitive-shape) is implicit and unused by callers; consumers see only Error.
line.fix: Merge SemanticFault into OperationFault as two additional methods. The single static class becomes the canonical Error factory for the Operation domain. Saves ~10 LOC of class declaration and import surface.

line.detail: Three sibling factories on OperationKey. `One(value)` wraps RequireValid into a Seq<TValue>. `Many(values)` aggregates RequireValid over an enumerable. `Solved(solved, value)` is `solved ? One(value) : Fin.Fail(...)`. Solved is one ternary line dressed as a function and has a single conceptual cost — short-circuit on a precomputed bool. CSP0708 ApiSurfaceInflationByPrefix risks tagging this family as inflated overload.
line.fix: Collapse via a single polymorphic entry: `key.Result(<discriminant>)` where the discriminant is a sealed DU `OperationOutcome<T>` with cases One(T), Many(IEnumerable<T>), Solved(bool, T). Keeps Domain semantics explicit and removes the 3-way naming proliferation.

line.detail: Computed is a static method on FoldExtensions that does NOT take its enclosing static class as receiver. CSP0506 ExtensionProjectionRequired flags 'static projection method over receiver must be an extension method'. The method is private so it does not strictly fail the rule (the rule scope likely targets internal/public surface), but the surface positioning of a non-extension static helper inside an extension class is asymmetric.
line.fix: Either inline (preferred — see C-04) or convert to extension `this (int Count, double Sum, double SumSquares, double Min, double Max) acc => ...`.

line.detail: Switch expression is the correct shape (CSP-compliant). However, two arms project trivially (`bool => Fin.Succ(value)`, `int => Fin.Succ(value)`, `Enum => Fin.Succ(value)`). These three arms collapse into a default-true branch. Additionally, IntersectionEvent at line 35-38 uses an unusual ValueTuple-equality pattern `(a, b, c) == (true, true, true)` which is correct but more obscure than `(a && b && c)`. The overall shape is fine; mentioned as a minor compression opportunity (~5 LOC).
line.fix: Combine bool|int|Enum into one default arm with type-switch or use an interface marker; tighten IntersectionEvent to a boolean conjunction. Saves ~5 LOC.

line.detail: CheckState is a private nested record-struct with three Fin<Unit> projection methods (Invalid, Require, RequireRhinoLog). Require and RequireRhinoLog are tiny gates (3 lines each) shared across the 13 GeometryCheck instances. The pattern is correct (no CSP violation), but the existence of CheckState only makes sense because each check captures an instance of `this` GeometryCheck — which means the check needs its own log key for ValidationFault.InvalidGeometry. Under the C-06 DU collapse, each check case becomes a record with its own validate method, and CheckState merges into the case method body.

SurfaceProfile is a private nested record-struct used only by SurfaceDomainReadiness and ContinuityReadiness (both in the same file). Two methods (HasUsableDomain, HasUsableContinuity). Single-use scoped helper that under the C-06 DU collapse moves into the SurfaceDomainReadiness/ContinuityReadiness cases as private members.

Three sibling validation projections delegate to GeometryValidation static methods. CSP0708 ApiSurfaceInflationByPrefix risks: Validate / ValidatePair / ValidateFirst is the same family pattern (Get/GetMany/GetOr) that the rule forbids. The variants discriminate on input shape (single, pair-both, pair-first-only).
line.fix: Collapse to a single polymorphic `Validate(GeometryShape shape)` where GeometryShape is a sealed DU with One(T)|Pair(TA,TB)|FirstOnly(TA,TB) cases. Each call site replaces the verb suffix with a discriminant constructor.

line.detail: Lambda body uses statement form `{ Brep brep = (Brep)state.Geometry; return ... switch { ... }; }`. CSP0001 ImperativeControlFlow targets statement-form bodies; the lambda contains an explicit declaration + return statement. The body is correct semantically but is statement-typed where expression-typed lambdas are doctrinally preferred.
line.fix: Convert to expression body via tuple deconstruction or pattern match: `static (CheckState state) => state.Geometry is Brep brep ? (...) switch { ... } : Fin.Succ(unit)`. Same applies at lines 187-198 (MeshRhinoCheck), 228-236 (CurveLengthReadiness), 242-252 (CurveAreaReadiness), 290-301 (PolycurveStructure), 306-318 (CurveSelfIntersection).

line.detail: Schedule.exponential | Schedule.recurs | Schedule.jitter is canonical LanguageExt 5 Schedule algebra (verified). The `|` is Schedule's Choice/Append (Or) operator. The configuration (50ms initial, 3 retries, 0.1 jitter) is plausible but `Schedule.upto(Duration)` is missing — without an upper time bound, the compounding exponential could exceed wall-clock budgets in pathological cases. Not a CSP violation, but a Schedule completeness gap.
line.fix: Add `& Schedule.upto(new Duration(seconds: 5.0))` (or whatever the SLO permits) to enforce an absolute timeout. The `&` operator is Schedule's Intersection — both must yield to retry.
